message:
fix(security): verify install.sh sha256 before exec; publish signed scripts; rich-output default-on

Two related supply-chain / UX changes that share src/cli.rs.

## install.sh sha256 verification (`git_safety_guard-ythp`)

`self_update_unix` previously did
  `curl -fsSL <script> | bash -s -- ...`
A tag-pinned but unverified pipe: a GitHub account compromise that
planted a malicious installer at the chosen tag would execute unchecked
on every user's machine running `dcg update`.

New flow:

1. Download install.sh to a tempdir (`mktemp -d -t dcg-install.XXXXXX`)
   with EXIT trap cleanup. No piping to bash.
2. Best-effort fetch `install.sh.sha256` from
   `releases/download/<tag>/install.sh.sha256`.
3. If present, verify with `shasum -a 256 -c` (preferred) or
   `sha256sum -c` (Alpine / distroless fallback). Both consume the
   `<hex>  <basename>` format `dist.yml` produces. If neither tool is
   available, warn and proceed (same posture as missing .sha256).
4. On verification mismatch: `echo "... FAILED — aborting." >&2; exit
   1`. The pre-existing tag-pinning still bounds the trust window even
   when verification is unavailable.
5. Tags published before this change have no `.sha256` artifact: the
   verifier emits a warning ("not published for this tag — proceeding
   without verification") and continues. This preserves the update
   path for stale binaries updating into the new world.
6. Only after verification (or the explicit no-sha warning) does the
   installer `bash $script $args` from disk.

`self_update_windows` mirrors the same flow with `Get-FileHash` against
`install.ps1.sha256`. Same fallbacks, same abort-on-mismatch posture.

CI side:

- `.github/workflows/dist.yml` adds a "Checksum + sign install scripts"
  step that runs after copying install.sh / install.ps1 into `dist/`.
  For each script, it produces `<script>.sha256` via `shasum -a 256
  <script>` and a cosign sigstore bundle (`<script>.sigstore.json`).
  Both files are uploaded with the rest of the release artifacts so
  `<release>/install.sh.sha256` is the URL the new verifier looks up.
- `set -euo pipefail` and `shopt -s nullglob` guard against partial
  artifact directories.

The accompanying eprintln messages in `self_update_*` are replaced —
the old WARNING text told users the script was unverified, which is no
longer true on tagged releases. New text: `"dcg update: downloading
and verifying install.sh from <tag>."` plus per-step success/fail
messages.

## Rich output default-on (`bd-15p0`)

Default `cargo build` / `cargo install` now ships with the `rich-output`
feature; lean/plain output is reachable via `cargo build
--no-default-features` for distros that need to drop the rich_rust
dependency. `legacy-output` placeholder feature is removed (it was a
no-op). Runtime plain output remains accessible through `DCG_NO_RICH=1`,
`NO_COLOR=1`, non-TTY auto-detection, and `--legacy-output`.

Because `rich-output` is now the default, `src/cli.rs`'s rich/plain
branching is collapsed: the previous `#[cfg(not(feature = "rich-
output"))]` branch becomes an unconditional fallback function (rich
helpers stay `#[cfg(feature = "rich-output")]`). Callers query
`crate::output::should_use_rich_output()` at runtime to choose between
the rich and plain renderer:

- `list_packs` and its `print_pack_patterns_plain` /
  `print_pack_pattern_lines` helpers are now always compiled; they
  either render directly (plain TTY / forced plain) or delegate to
  `list_packs_rich` (TTY, no NO_COLOR, no DCG_NO_RICH).
- `print_scan_pretty` is split into `print_scan_pretty_plain` and
  `print_scan_pretty_rich` with a runtime dispatcher that picks based
  on `should_use_rich_output()`.
- `handle_explain` Pretty branch routes to a new
  `print_explain_pretty_plain` for the non-rich path; the existing
  `print_explain_regex_line` becomes always-compiled.
- `handle_stats_command`, `handle_stats_rules`, and `doctor` get the
  same rich-or-plain dispatcher treatment.

`format_rule_metrics_pretty`, `markdown_single_line_for_cli`, and
`doctor_pretty` lose their `#[cfg(not(feature = "rich-output"))]`
gates — they're always part of the build now and selected at runtime.

CI: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` adds a `cargo check --all-targets
--no-default-features` step so the lean build doesn't silently break
when rich-output gating is removed from a function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -124,6 +124,9 @@ jobs:
       - name: Check compilation
         run: cargo check --all-targets
 
+      - name: Check lean build without rich output
+        run: cargo check --all-targets --no-default-features
+
       - name: Build release binary for tests
         run: cargo build --release

@@ -130,6 +130,23 @@ jobs:
           cp install.sh dist/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No install.sh found"
           cp install.ps1 dist/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No install.ps1 found"
 
+      - name: Checksum + sign install scripts (git_safety_guard-ythp)
+        # Publishes install.sh.sha256 / install.ps1.sha256 (with the
+        # `<sha>  <basename>` format `shasum -a 256 -c` understands) plus
+        # cosign sigstore bundles. `dcg update` downloads the script to a
+        # tempfile, fetches the sha256, verifies, and only then exec's it.
+        # Without this the installer-fetch step is a supply-chain trust
+        # window even when pinned to a tag.
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          shopt -s nullglob
+          cd dist
+          for script in install.sh install.ps1; do
+            [ -f "$script" ] || continue
+            shasum -a 256 "$script" > "$script.sha256"
+            cosign sign-blob --yes --bundle "$script.sigstore.json" "$script"
+          done
+
       - name: Create GitHub Release
         uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v3
         with:

@@ -133,9 +133,9 @@ strip = true        # Remove debug symbols
 opt-level = 1       # Faster compile times during development
 
 [features]
+default = ["rich-output"]
 rayon = ["dep:rayon"]
 rich-output = ["dep:rich_rust"]  # Enable rich_rust for premium terminal output
-legacy-output = []               # Keep old rendering (placeholder for gradual migration)
 
 [lints.rust]
 # unsafe_code = "forbid" # Moved to src/lib.rs and src/main.rs to allow unsafe in tests
